heading: (intro)
raw: Written and directed by David Lynch.
clean: Written and directed by David Lynch.

heading: Henry Spencer
raw: [the Baby is going into violent convulsions and has broken out in spots] Oh, you are sick!
clean: Oh, you are sick!

heading: Others
raw: Beautiful Girl Across the Hall: I locked myself out of my apartment... [pause] and it's so late.
clean: I locked myself out of my apartment... and it's so late.

heading: Others
raw: Pencil Factory Manager: [exasperated from his assistant's constant buzzing] OKAY, PAUL!
clean: OKAY, PAUL!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Beautiful Girl Across the Hall: Are you Henry?
clean: Are you Henry?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Beautiful Girl Across the Hall: A girl named "Mary" called on the payphone in the hallway about an hour ago. She said that she's at her parents' and that you're invited to dinner.
clean: A girl named "Mary" called on the payphone in the hallway about an hour ago. She said that she's at her parents' and that you're invited to dinner.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Oh, yeah? [after a long pause] Well... thank you very much.
clean: Oh, yeah? Well... thank you very much.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: You're late, Henry.
clean: You're late, Henry.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: I didn't know if you wanted me to come or not. Where have you been? You never come around anymore.
clean: I didn't know if you wanted me to come or not. Where have you been? You never come around anymore.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: It's Henry isn't it?
clean: It's Henry isn't it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Mary tells me you're a very nice fellow. What do you do?
clean: Mary tells me you're a very nice fellow. What do you do?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Oh, I'm on vacation.
clean: Oh, I'm on vacation.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: What did you do?
clean: What did you do?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: I work at LaPelle's Factory. I'm a printer.
clean: I work at LaPelle's Factory. I'm a printer.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: Hen-Henry's very clever at printing.
clean: Hen-Henry's very clever at printing.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Yes, he sounds very clever.
clean: Yes, he sounds very clever.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: I thought I heard a stranger. We've got chicken tonight. Strangest damn things. They're man-made. Little damn things, smaller than my fist - but they're new!...... I'm Bill.
clean: I thought I heard a stranger. We've got chicken tonight. Strangest damn things. They're man-made. Little damn things, smaller than my fist - but they're new!...... I'm Bill.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Hello. I'm Henry.
clean: Hello. I'm Henry.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Henry's at Lappell's factory.
clean: Henry's at Lappell's factory.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: So, printin's yer business, eh? Plumbin's mine. Thirty years! I've watched this neighborhood change from pastures to the hell-hole it is now! I put every damn pipe in this town!
clean: So, printin's yer business, eh? Plumbin's mine. Thirty years! I've watched this neighborhood change from pastures to the hell-hole it is now! I put every damn pipe in this town!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: Dad!..
clean: Dad!..

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Bill...
clean: Bill...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: People think pipes grow in their homes! Well they sure as hell don't. Look at my knees! Look at my knees!
clean: People think pipes grow in their homes! Well they sure as hell don't. Look at my knees! Look at my knees!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Bill...
clean: Bill...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: Are ya hungry?
clean: Are ya hungry?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Bill...
clean: Bill...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: Well Henry, what do you know?
clean: Well Henry, what do you know?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Oh, I don't know much of anything.
clean: Oh, I don't know much of anything.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: Mary usually does the carving but tonight since you are our guest, you could do it, Henry. All right with you?
clean: Mary usually does the carving but tonight since you are our guest, you could do it, Henry. All right with you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Of course. I'd be happy to. [as the chickens begin moving] So I just, uh... I just cut them up like regular chickens?
clean: Of course. I'd be happy to. So I just, uh... I just cut them up like regular chickens?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mr. X: Sure, just cut them up like regular chickens. Note: this quote is sampled by Amon Tobin on Permutation (album), for the track "Like Regular Chickens".
clean: Sure, just cut them up like regular chickens. Note: this quote is sampled by Amon Tobin on Permutation (album), for the track "Like Regular Chickens".

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Henry, may I speak to you a minute? Over here. Did you and Mary have sexual intercourse?
clean: Henry, may I speak to you a minute? Over here. Did you and Mary have sexual intercourse?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Did you?
clean: Did you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Why are you asking me this question?
clean: Why are you asking me this question?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: I have a very good reason, and now I want you to tell me.
clean: I have a very good reason, and now I want you to tell me.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: I'm, I'm very... I love Mary!
clean: I'm, I'm very... I love Mary!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Henry, I asked you if you and Mary had sexual intercourse!
clean: Henry, I asked you if you and Mary had sexual intercourse!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Well, I don't... I don't think that's any of your business!
clean: Well, I don't... I don't think that's any of your business!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Henry!
clean: Henry!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: I'm sorry.
clean: I'm sorry.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: You're in very bad trouble if you won't cooperate... [nuzzling at his neck]
clean: You're in very bad trouble if you won't cooperate...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Well, I... Mary!
clean: Well, I... Mary!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: [grabbing her away] Mother! [sobs]
clean: Mother!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: Answer me!
clean: Answer me!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: I'm too nervous.
clean: I'm too nervous.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: There's a baby. It's at the hospital.
clean: There's a baby. It's at the hospital.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mrs. X: And you're the father.
clean: And you're the father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Well that's impossible! It's only been...
clean: Well that's impossible! It's only been...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: Mother, they're still not sure it is a baby!
clean: Mother, they're still not sure it is a baby!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: You wouldn't mind marrying me, would you Henry?
clean: You wouldn't mind marrying me, would you Henry?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Well... No.
clean: Well... No.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: [to the crying baby] Shut up! [Baby continues to cry] I can't take it anymore! I'm going home!
clean: Shut up! I can't take it anymore! I'm going home!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: What are you talking about?
clean: What are you talking about?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: All I need is a decent night's sleep!
clean: All I need is a decent night's sleep!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: Why don't you just stay home...
clean: Why don't you just stay home...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Mary X: I'll do what I want! And you better take good care of things while I'm gone!
clean: I'll do what I want! And you better take good care of things while I'm gone!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Beautiful Girl Across the Hall: Where's your wife?
clean: Where's your wife?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Henry: She must have gone back to her parents again. I'm not sure.
clean: She must have gone back to her parents again. I'm not sure.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Beautiful Girl Across the Hall: Can I spend the night here?
clean: Can I spend the night here?
